codeReview.summary: Feature behavior is largely implemented: empty states and CTAs are present on the targeted dashboard pages, loading skeletons were added for the requested surfaces, and a route-level App Router error boundary was added for the dashboard segment. I found only non-blocking implementation gaps.

issues.description: The feature description explicitly called for using the shared `Skeleton` component from `components/ui`, but the analytics loading UI uses page-local `div` blocks with `animate-pulse` instead of `Skeleton`.

The new class-based `ErrorBoundary` is created and exported, but there are no usages in `src/`; runtime fallback currently relies on `(dashboard)/error.tsx` only. This leaves the class boundary as unused code.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill requires agent-browser verification for UI features, but this worker transcript shows no agent-browser invocation while `handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is still true. This suggests either procedural drift in execution or that the skill/feedback expectations need tightening.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md lines 23, 33, 52 require agent-browser/manual UI verification; transcript skeleton for worker session 8440d550-55db-42bb-9c2e-ba079f3f5d81 shows Skill calls for mission-worker-base and fullstack-worker only, with no agent-browser usage.

summary: Reviewed commit 2cdba6d for feature empty-states-loading-skeletons-error-boundaries. Core UX behavior is present and acceptable for pass, with two non-blocking gaps: inconsistent skeleton-component usage and an unused class-based ErrorBoundary. Also noted a skill-process mismatch around required agent-browser verification.
